title: salix × fontqueri
intro: Salix × fontqueri, also known as Fontquer's willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the crossing of Salix fragilis and Salix atrocinerea. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × fontqueri include its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The herbal preparations of Salix × fontqueri include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or mixing it with other herbs to create a topical application.
